text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
GYNECOLOGIC PATHOLOGY DIVISION

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Delacroix
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION #: SP-[REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: Gynecologic Oncology Service
PROCEDURE: Diagnostic Laparoscopy with Peritoneal Biopsy and Omental Core Biopsy
CLINICAL INDICATION: Bilateral complex adnexal masses with large-volume ascites and peritoneal carcinomatosis on imaging; elevated CA-125 (1,847 U/mL); rule out primary ovarian malignancy vs. other primary site
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Right pelvic peritoneal implant biopsy (forceps biopsy, laparoscopic)
  B. Omental core biopsy (Tru-cut needle biopsy of omental cake, laparoscopic)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A - Right Pelvic Peritoneal Implant:
Received in formalin, labeled "right pelvic peritoneum biopsy," are multiple irregular gray-white to tan firm tissue fragments, in aggregate measuring 1.4 x 0.9 x 0.3 cm. Cut surfaces are homogeneously tan-white with a firm, fibrous consistency. No cystic areas, no necrosis, and no grossly discernible hemorrhage. Entirely submitted in one cassette.

Specimen B - Omental Core Biopsy:
Received in formalin, labeled "omental biopsy," are two core needle biopsy fragments, each measuring approximately 1.2 cm in length and 0.2 cm in diameter, tan-white, firm to rubbery in texture, with a heterogeneous cut surface demonstrating areas of gray-white firm tissue alternating with pale yellow adipose tissue. Both cores entirely submitted in one cassette.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A - Right Pelvic Peritoneal Implant:
Sections show fibrous peritoneal tissue extensively infiltrated by carcinoma arranged in complex papillary and micropapillary fronds, solid nests, and slit-like glandular spaces. The papillary structures are lined by markedly atypical epithelial cells with high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios, markedly irregular nuclear contours, coarse chromatin, and conspicuous eosinophilic macronucleoli. Cytoplasm is scant to moderate and eosinophilic. Mitotic figures are numerous and readily identifiable, including atypical forms; mitotic count exceeds 10 per 10 high-power fields. Several psammoma bodies (concentrically laminated calcifications) are identified within the papillary cores. A desmoplastic stromal reaction is present at the peritoneal interface. There is no evidence of mucinous differentiation, clear cell morphology, or endometrioid features. No lymphovascular invasion is identified in this limited specimen.

Specimen B - Omental Core Biopsy:
Sections of omental adipose tissue show near-complete replacement by carcinoma with identical morphology to Specimen A. The tumor demonstrates papillary and solid architecture with slit-like spaces characteristic of high-grade serous carcinoma. Nuclear atypia is severe with marked pleomorphism, irregular chromatin distribution, and large prominent nucleoli. Mitotic rate is high (>10/10 HPF). Occasional psammoma bodies are noted. Extensive desmoplastic fibrosis surrounds nests and sheets of tumor cells. Background omental fat is present at the periphery of the cores, confirming omental origin. No necrosis identified in these small biopsy specimens. No mucinous or clear cell features.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES - IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

Immunohistochemistry was performed on Specimen A (right pelvic peritoneal implant) using validated antibody clones on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue sections with appropriate positive and negative controls.

  WT1 (clone WT49):                POSITIVE - diffuse, strong nuclear staining (3+) in >90% of tumor cells
  PAX8 (clone MRQ-50):             POSITIVE - diffuse, strong nuclear staining (3+)
  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):        POSITIVE - diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):             NEGATIVE - no staining in tumor cells (positive internal control: colonic mucosa)
  p53 (clone DO-7):                ABERRANT OVEREXPRESSION PATTERN - diffuse, strong nuclear staining in >80% of tumor cells (consistent with TP53 missense "gain-of-function" mutation; this staining pattern is distinct from the null/complete absence pattern seen with truncating mutations)
  ER (estrogen receptor, clone SP1): POSITIVE - nuclear staining in approximately 30% of tumor cells (H-score approximately 40; low-level expression)
  PR (progesterone receptor, clone 16):  NEGATIVE - no nuclear staining
  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):             POSITIVE - proliferative index approximately 75% of tumor nuclei
  BerEP4 (EpCAM):                  POSITIVE - diffuse membranous staining
  Calretinin:                      NEGATIVE (negative in tumor cells; supports carcinoma over mesothelioma)
  D2-40 (podoplanin):              NEGATIVE in tumor cells
  Napsin A:                        NEGATIVE
  TTF-1:                           NEGATIVE
  CDX2:                            NEGATIVE

INTERPRETATION OF IHC: The immunoprofile (WT1+, PAX8+, CK7+, CK20-, p53 aberrant overexpression, ER low-positive, PR negative, calretinin negative, napsin A negative, TTF-1 negative, CDX2 negative) is consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin (ovarian/fallopian tube/primary peritoneal). The aberrant p53 overexpression pattern is a hallmark of TP53 mutation, which is present in >96% of high-grade serous carcinomas. This profile effectively excludes primary mucinous carcinoma, mesothelioma, metastatic colorectal carcinoma, lung adenocarcinoma, and other common peritoneal tumor types.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT PELVIC PERITONEAL IMPLANT BIOPSY:
   - HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
   - Morphology and immunophenotype consistent with primary ovarian/fallopian tube/peritoneal origin (Mullerian)
   - See ancillary studies and note below

B. OMENTAL CORE BIOPSY:
   - HIGH-GRADE SEROUS CARCINOMA
   - Morphology identical to Specimen A
   - Extensive replacement of omental adipose tissue by carcinoma

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC NOTE:

The histologic features and immunohistochemical profile of both specimens are diagnostic of high-grade serous carcinoma (HGSC). The combination of complex papillary architecture, slit-like spaces, prominent psammoma bodies, severe nuclear atypia (FIGO nuclear grade 3), high mitotic index (>10/10 HPF), and the characteristic p53 aberrant overexpression pattern fulfills established morphologic criteria for HGSC per the WHO Classification of Tumours of the Female Genital Tract.

In the setting of bilateral complex adnexal masses with peritoneal carcinomatosis and the clinical/radiographic picture provided, the findings are most consistent with primary ovarian or fallopian tube high-grade serous carcinoma with peritoneal dissemination. Definitive assignment of primary site (ovary vs. fallopian tube vs. primary peritoneal) will require examination of the primary surgical specimen (ovaries, fallopian tubes) at the time of definitive cytoreductive surgery.

The low-level estrogen receptor positivity (approximately 30%, H-score ~40) is within the range commonly observed in HGSC and does not alter the diagnosis or histologic classification.

Molecular testing (germline BRCA1/BRCA2 and somatic tumor profiling) is clinically indicated for this diagnosis per current guidelines.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE:
Attending Pathologist: [Gynecologic Pathology Attending, MD, PhD]
Resident/Fellow: [Gynecologic Pathology Fellow, MD]
Report Status: FINAL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
